title: Investment Manager Sheds $45.7 Million Worth of Software Stock, According to Latest SEC Filing
author: Jake Lerch
description: Conestoga Capital Advisors sold 634,534 shares of SPS Commerce (SPSC) worth approximately $45.7 million in Q1 2026, reducing its stake to near-negligible levels. The stock has declined 59.8% over the past year but trades at a P/S ratio of 2.8x—the lowest in over a decade—while the company maintains solid revenue growth and invests in AI capabilities.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Mixed signals: negative price performance (down 59.8% YoY) and major institutional stake reduction suggest weakness, but the company shows resilient revenue growth ($492M to $750M), attractive valuation metrics (2.8x P/S near decade lows), and strategic AI investments. The sale appears tactical rather than a vote of no confidence.
Mentioned as Conestoga Capital's top holding ($233.17M, 4.7% of AUM) with no specific news or performance data provided in the article.
Listed as second-largest holding in Conestoga Capital's portfolio ($222.51M, 4.4% of AUM) with no specific news or performance data provided.

title: Garrett Motion adds a turbocharged twist to Carmel's AI-heavy portfolio
author: Seena Hassouna
description: Carmel Capital Management acquired 314,750 shares of Garrett Motion (GTX) for approximately $5.87 million in Q1 2026, representing a new 1.6% position in the fund. The acquisition fits within Carmel's broader industrial and automotive supply chain strategy, complementing existing holdings in auto chips, aftermarket parts, and aerospace propulsion. GTX shares have surged 129% over the past year, significantly outperforming the S&P 500.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong 129% year-over-year stock performance, outperforming S&P 500 by 98.46 percentage points. New institutional investment from Carmel Capital suggests confidence in the company's positioning in the evolving automotive landscape with turbocharging and electric-boosting technologies.
Top holding in Carmel's portfolio at $43.80 million (12.5% of AUM), representing core AI infrastructure exposure.

title: Port Capital Doubles Down on Atmus Filtration Technologies Stock, Buys Another $22 Million in Shares
author: Josh Kohn-Lindquist
description: Port Capital LLC increased its stake in Atmus Filtration Technologies by 446,260 shares worth approximately $21.6 million. The position now represents 1.96% of the fund's AUM. The investment follows Atmus's $450 million acquisition of Koch Filter, expanding into high-growth markets like data centers and power generation driven by AI demand.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock has appreciated 55.7% over the past year with 36 percentage points of alpha versus S&P 500. Port Capital's continued investment signals confidence. The Koch Filter acquisition provides exposure to high-growth AI-driven markets (data centers, power generation). Trading at reasonable 20x forward earnings with stable recurring revenue streams and HALO characteristics make it attractive for long-term growth.
Mentioned as Port Capital's top holding (9.4% of AUM) but no new information or activity reported in this article.

title: ESCO Completes Divestiture Of VACCO Industries
author: Kate Lowrey
description: ESCO Technologies has sold VACCO Industries to RBC Bearings for approximately $275 million, completing a previously announced transaction with customary working capital adjustments.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company is strategically divesting a business unit, which suggests portfolio optimization rather than financial distress
Successfully acquired a new business unit for $275 million, expanding their portfolio of engineered products

title: Ball Bearing Market Growth Expected to Reach USD 33.24 Billion by 2032 at 3.5% CAGR From 2024-2032 | Introspective Market Research
author: Introspective Market Research
description: The global Ball Bearing Market is expected to grow from $25.24 billion in 2023 to $33.24 billion by 2032, at a CAGR of 3.5% from 2024-2032. The growth is driven by rising demand for machinery and equipment in various industries, as well as advancements in technology and manufacturing.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The Timken Company is mentioned as one of the key manufacturers in the ball bearing market, indicating its strong presence and potential to benefit from the market growth.
RBC Bearings Inc is mentioned as one of the key manufacturers in the ball bearing market, indicating its strong presence and potential to benefit from the market growth.
